title: hieracium murorum subsp. incisum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. incisum, also known as the Wall Hawkweed or Cut-leaved Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. incisum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
